Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to list all the activities SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS Thats 10 activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The temporal profile needs to include pairs where one activity eventually follows another in at least one trace

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a typical supply chain the order is probably linear SS to OP to RC to QI to CA to PT to PK to WS to DT to AS So in that case all pairs where the first is before the second in this sequence would be valid

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: But the user says a representative subset so I do not have to do all possible pairs Maybe pick some key ones including some that are far apart like SS to DT and some closer ones

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets think about realistic time frames for each step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Starting with SS Supplier Selection How long does it take to select a supplier Maybe a few days

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But maybe SS is quick Maybe it is a matter of hours Wait but in reality supplier selection for a new order might involve checking availability prices etc Maybe 1 to 2 days Lets go with 1 day

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Next OP Order Placement After selecting supplier placing the order Maybe this is quick like a few hours

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Then RC Receive Components After placing order how long to receive Depends on supplier lead time Maybe 7 days average

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: QI After receiving inspecting Maybe 1 day

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: CA Assembling Maybe 2 days

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: PT Testing might take a day

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: PK Maybe 6 hours

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: WS This could vary a lot Maybe average 3 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: AS is ongoing but the time between DT and AS might be the duration until AS starts

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: This is a bit ambiguous But in process mining typically each activity has a start and end but here the temporal profile is about the time between the end of one activity and the start of the next or between the starts

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the time between A and C is the sum of A to B and B to C then the average of A to C is avg A B plus avg B C and the variance is var A B plus var B C assuming independence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thats a key point

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore if I define the consecutive pairs first then I can compute the non consecutive pairs based on those

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Consecutive pairs SS OP OP RC RC QI QI CA CA PT PT PK PK WS WS DT DT AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now estimate each of these

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Revised consecutive pairs SS OP 12 hours OP RC 10 days RC QI 1 day QI CA 2 days CA PT 3 days PT PK 1 day PK WS 6 hours WS DT 5 days DT AS 7 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now for skip one SS RC is SS OP plus OP RC OP QI is OP RC plus RC QI RC CA is RC QI plus QI CA

Name: Validation - PE
Text: These look good

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The user wants a representative subset so we need to include some consecutive pairs some with one step in between some with multiple steps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets choose all 9 consecutive pairs then some skip one some skip two and a couple of longer skips

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This gives a good mix consecutive skip one skip two and longer skips covering different parts of the process
